sections.paragraph:
constr. from כָּהוּ (like קְטָלוּ from קְטָלָהוּ, see Hebr. Gramm. ed. 10, p. 24, 82), pr. like as this, i.q. כָּקֶה, Arab. كَذَا i.e.

(1) so, thus, Gen 32:5 Exo 3:15. Of very frequent occurrence is the phrase כֹּה אָמַר פּ׳, where the words themselves follow, Jud. 11:15 especially in the beginning of communications from God. כֹּה אָמַר יְהֹוָה, “thus saith Jehovah,” Jer 2:2 7:20 9:16 , 22 . Rarely in the manner of substantives with a prefix בְּכֹה in this manner (like בְּכֵן for כֵּן), and put twice in this manner … in that manner, 1Ki 22:20.

(2) When applied to place, hither, here, but this is rare, Gen 31:37 2Sa 18:30 also doubled, here, there; hither, thither, Num 11:31. עַד־כֹּה hitherto, dorthin, Gen 22:5. כֹּה וָכֹה hither, and thither, Exo 2:12.

(3) Used of time, now עַד־כֹּה hitherto, Exo 7:16 Jos 17:14. עַד־כֹּה וְעַד־כֹּה till now and till then, bis dann und dann, i.e. in the meanwhile; 1Ki 18:45.
